name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) will take place in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers a one‑week program of introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials on Bilateral AI, and a Digital Humanism track, targeting PhD students, young researchers, and senior scholars. The school also includes social events such as a reception at the Vienna City Hall and is co‑located with the 5th ACM Europe Digital Humanism Summer School.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is organized by the Nordic Language Processing Laboratory and the University of Oslo and takes place at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries, and offers in‑depth technical presentations, hands‑on elements, and discussions on multilingual large language models, evaluation, and data curation. The event is subsidized by the OpenEuroLLM project, provides free participation and bus transfers, and includes networking opportunities with European research teams.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The Probabilistic AI School 2026 will be held in Vilnius, Lithuania from August 3 to August 7, 2026. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment featuring state‑of‑the‑art expertise in machine learning and artificial intelligence for enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals. The school is co‑organized with the Artificial Intelligence Association of Lithuania (AIAL), offering participants networking opportunities and exposure to cutting‑edge research.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a broad range of computer‑vision topics, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for participants seeking advanced knowledge and networking opportunities in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: Oxford ML School, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 offers three specialised tracks in AI theory and applications, taught by world‑renowned experts from leading academic and industry labs. The programme is hosted by the Oxford ML School and can be attended in Oxford or online, allowing participants to customise their learning path. It aims to upskill participants with cutting‑edge knowledge and flexible track selection.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It brings together doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to discuss topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making through keynotes, industry talks, brainstorming sessions and student presentations. The program is hosted at the Happy Tammsvik resort and includes indoor and outdoor ice‑breaker activities, with all accommodation and meals sponsored for participants.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held at the National Robotarium, Heriot‑Watt University in Edinburgh, United Kingdom. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI for robotics, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art technology. The school targets master’s‑level students and industry practitioners, providing networking opportunities and a special dinner.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted by Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering intensive lectures by 20 international experts, hands‑on sessions, and the possibility of scholarships and a short‑paper presentation.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems is held at Umeå University in Sweden. It offers lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session and a social program aimed at researchers, students and professionals interested in the societal implications of AI and AS. Participants can network, exchange ideas, and receive guidance on ethical, legal and societal challenges while staying in Umeå during March 11‑13, 2026.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The SoRAIM'27 Winter School focuses on social robotics, artificial intelligence, and multimedia, bringing together researchers from computer vision, speech processing, affective computing, and human‑robot interaction. It is primarily aimed at PhD students and early‑career researchers, though participants from industry and academia at all levels are welcome. The programme combines lectures, hands‑on sessions, and informal networking in a mountain setting near Grenoble, offering both scientific exchange and outdoor activities.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 NLDL Winter School is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference and will be held in Tromsø, Norway. It runs from 11 January to 15 January 2027 and focuses on recent advances in deep learning, including image classification, object detection, and speech recognition. The school targets researchers and practitioners who wish to exchange ideas, foster collaborations, and learn from leading experts in the field.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work. It targets senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals from Nepal and abroad. The school aims to provide fundamental AI knowledge, expose participants to state‑of‑the‑art research, and foster networking among more than 500 in‑person attendees and thousands of online viewers.

name: EEML Summer School
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. The program features lectures and tutorials by leading experts from academia and industry, covering topics such as deep learning, reinforcement learning, and AI applications. It is aimed at graduate students and early‑career researchers in the region who seek intensive training and networking opportunities.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program featuring keynotes, lectures, and hands‑on sessions led by AI experts. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, covering topics such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models, reinforcement learning and ethics in machine learning. The school emphasizes networking, poster presentations, and diversity through scholarships, and the next edition will be held in Spain in September 2027.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day immersive program held at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco. It offers young researchers, Ph.D. students, post‑docs and professionals a mix of theoretical lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work delivered by world‑renowned experts in multimodal and generative AI. The programme also emphasizes networking and leadership development to build the next generation of AI leaders in Morocco.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing (AthNLP) Summer School 2026 will be held at the campus of NCSR Demokritos in Athens, Greece. The program, organized by a consortium of research centers and universities, focuses on advanced topics in natural language processing and offers hands‑on tutorials and lectures. It runs from September 2 to 8, 2026 and is positioned alongside the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Greece.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school at Lady Margaret Hall covers generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on projects on clustering, GANs, self‑supervised and energy‑based models, and is offered both residentially and online. The programme is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML experience who are preparing for graduate study or research.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI is a comprehensive program that explores the intersection of artificial intelligence and scientific research. It offers hands‑on workshops, case studies, and expert discussions covering machine learning for data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and integration of AI with simulations. The school is aimed at PhD students, post‑doctoral researchers, and scientists familiar with mathematical modelling, numerical computation, and Python programming.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive program held at LUT University’s Lappeenranta campus, offering courses that combine sustainability, climate action, marketing, management and inventive thinking across business and technology. The programme is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students from any university who wish to earn 3 ECTS credits while engaging in workshops, group projects and a vibrant social programme in the Finnish summer. Courses are delivered on‑site from 3 to 7 August 2026, with some online components, and participants benefit from access to campus facilities, temporary LUT accounts and networking opportunities.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: AIces 2026 is the inaugural International School on the Cognitive, Ethical and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence, held in Porto–Maia, Portugal. The four‑day event (30 March–2 April 2026) offers parallel courses, a symposium, and open‑debate sessions for graduate students, post‑doctoral researchers and industry practitioners interested in AI foundations, ethics and societal impact. Participants can attend face‑to‑face or online, receive recordings for 45 days, and earn a certificate of participation.
